channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Satya Nadella has issued a shocking warning to companies using AI
item.description: In a surprising blog post on Monday, Microsoft CEO is warning enterprises of the dangers of using proprietary models like Anthropic's and OpenAI's.

item.title: The Shokz OpenRun Pro are the cheapest they’ve been since January
item.description: Noise-canceling earbuds are great for flights and focusing, but they're not always ideal for outdoor workouts. The last-gen Shokz OpenRun Pro's open-ear design lets you enjoy music while staying aware of approaching cars, cyclists, and other potential hazards, and right now they're on sale for $109 ($50 off) at Amazon, Walmart, and B&H Photo. That […]...

item.title: How to download the iOS 27 public beta — and 3 new things to try first
item.description: Siri has finally got smarter with iOS 27, and the public beta is out now. Here's how to download it and the best features to try first.

item.title: Watch out Windows users, a Secure Boot update has been blocked on Windows 11 PCs due to failing on some devices — here's how to check if you're affected
item.description: Certain older devices that cannot receive updates via their OEM may not be able to install the latest Secure Boot certificate.

item.title: The Pixel colors might rule this year
item.description: This year's Google Pixel 11 lineup might come in a bunch of funky colors. A series of now-deleted Amazon listings spotted by 9to5Google show what appear to be placeholders for Google's upcoming Pixel 11 in hot pink Fuchsia (Hibiscus), vibrant green Moss (Pistachio), and Midnight (Obsidian) black. We've seen two sets of names for the […]

item.title: Apple’s public betas for iOS 27 and more are out now
item.description: Apple has just released public betas for iOS 27 and other major OS updates that are set to publicly launch this fall. The big new feature this year is Siri AI, the delayed AI-powered revamp to Siri. It actually works - which is big praise! - though it keeps things brief. Other betas available now […]

item.title: Public betas for iOS 27, macOS 27 and more Apple platforms are now available
item.description: Try the new Siri AI and system-wide performance improvements.

item.title: Siri AI makes the Apple Watch finally feel like a wrist computer
item.description: Siri has been on the Apple Watch since day one, though I'm usually hard-pressed to find people who actually make good use of it. It's kind of just… been there - mostly as a way to set timers when my hands are full. But after playing around with the watchOS 27 developer beta, I get […]

item.title: Siri AI is already changing how I use my iPhone
item.description: iOS 27 escaped the developer world today with the launch of the first public beta. I've been testing the new operating system since early June, looking for quirks and seeing if it can live up to the hype Apple promised in the keynote. This year's iOS upgrades are what one might call a Snow Leopard […]

item.title: The macOS 27 public beta is worth it just for the Liquid Glass tweaks
item.description: The macOS 27 Golden Gate public beta is here, and anyone with an M-series Mac now has easier access to test-drive Apple's latest changes - including a more subdued Liquid Glass aesthetic. That's reason enough to be at least a little excited for macOS 27 (particularly if you're on Tahoe and disliking all the transparency). […]

item.title: Your next budget problem may not be electricity—it’s your water bill
item.description: A new Stanford study finds climate-related infrastructure projects could cause residential water bills to double in some parts of the U.S. Brace yourself for higher water bills—and soon.

item.title: Space Force has a new weapon to target enemy satellites — Meadowlands electromagnetic beam marks a 'huge milestone' in US capability
item.description: Electromagnetic warfare is now mobile, and Space Force has the capability to cripple enemy satellites.

item.title: Los Angeles law enforcement will stop using Flock cameras
item.description: The Los Angeles police department did not renew its contract with Flock due to data privacy concerns.

item.title: Apple says former employee exploited ‘rare’ bug to download confidential files after leaving for OpenAI
item.description: Apple would not comment on the "security breach," which allegedly allowed a former employee to download sensitive files from Apple's network long after he departed the company for rival OpenAI.

item.title: Chipotle’s COO looks for four key traits when promoting leaders
item.description: Jason Kidd visits several Chipotle stores a week and has dinners with regional managers to identify potential candidates for internal promotions, Business Insider reports. Last year, 23,000 Chipotle employees were promoted to various leadership roles.

item.title: The Problem With VAR at the 2026 World Cup Isn’t the Technology—It’s Who Interprets It
item.description: The video assistant referee system, or VAR, has led to some controversial calls at the 2026 World Cup. Here’s why.

item.title: A ransomware negotiator who colluded with attackers to scam victims was sentenced to 70 months in prison
item.description: Angelo Martino helped scam ransomware victims out of over $75 million, officials said.

item.title: People think Mitch McConnell’s hospital photo is AI—and AI isn’t helping
item.description: As photographic evidence loses its power to convince, some social media users are turning to chatbots for answers. Are they making the conspiracies worse? From bunnies jumping on a trampoline at night to Norwegian soccer player Erling Haaland getting scared of his own reflection, the more that AI-generated content makes its way into our social feeds, the harder it becomes to tell what’s false from reality. ...

item.title: Health equity is a social justice issue
item.description: Organizations can’t sit it out. Health equity can sometimes be discussed in terms of access, but at its core, it is a social justice issue. It reflects who has been systematically excluded from the conditions that make health possible: safe housing, stable income, reliable employment, clean environments, and freedom from targeted harm.

item.title: This AI-powered shape-shifting wing could make aircraft tails obsolete — and slash travel costs
item.description: The German Aerospace Center is developing a 1 million Euro project exploring the use of morphing wings, redefining how we think of airplanes.

item.title: Here’s how America builds again
item.description: We need new blueprints to scale production, workforce capability, and industrial competitiveness simultaneously. For anyone in manufacturing or fulfillment, the current topic of conversation is Factory 5.0, also called Industry 5.0.

item.title: Un nouveau lien mystérieux entre la théorie de la chaleur et les trous noirs !
item.description: Il y a environ 50 ans, Stephen Hawking et son collègue Jacob Bekenstein établissaient un lien profond entre la thermodynamique, qui gouverne l'Univers des étoiles aux réactions chimiques de la vie, et les trous noirs, ces manifestations spectaculaires de la courbure de l'espace-temps d'Einstein....
